返回本批次

伦敦人厌倦了有关他们城市的谎言的病毒视频

Londoners are sick of viral videos telling lies about their city

www.londoncentric.media 作者 pseudolus 分数 76 评论 83 2026/04/07 02:31
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. London Centric 倾向于每周发布两次，以避免堵塞您的收件箱。有时我们会给您带来一项需要花费数月时间的调查。有时我们会在上面撒上一些新闻，或者正在进行的故事的更新。有时，就像今天一样，我们想为您带来一切。请享受这个周末更新，其中充满了独家故事。保证当你向下滚动时会有一些你在其他地方没有读过的东西——否则你的退款。新的调查将于下周进行。有我们应该调查的故事吗？通过电子邮件或 WhatsApp 联系我们。感谢所有联系我们的读者，分享他们在世界上一些最偏远的角落（我将其定义为从苏里南到萨里的地区）遇到的人们的故事，他们认为伦敦正处于社会全面崩溃的状态。这是关于未来的 TikTok 企业家的故事，他说他决定专门制作有关伦敦房屋被送给非法移民的虚假视频，因为“仇恨带来观点”。周五晚上，伦敦警察局告诉我们，他们正在继续调查 TikTok 帐户背后的个人是否犯有罪行，该人错误地认为自己发布的内容没有任何潜在后果。越来越明显的是，伦敦正在遭受一场全球声誉危机，病毒式视频网站的激励加剧了这一危机。它影响着首都的旅游业、投资以及城市的总体幸福感。它由合法的原始视频驱动，这些视频以高度明显的手机盗窃和暴力抢劫为特色。然后，不择手段的内容创作者和社交媒体平台又夸大了这一点，他们鼓励人们对所谓的城市社会彻底崩溃进行煽动。最遥远遭遇的第一名是伦敦中心读者乔·琼斯：2025年11月，我去苏里南度假。我们乘坐这架小型飞机飞往亚马逊雨林的一个偏远丛林前哨，并在那里停留了五天。这架小型八座飞机每周往返两次，接送客人和运送物资，完全脱离电网。而且住在那里的客人无法上网。它是由一个大约 60 岁的苏里南人经营的。他在那里工作了大约 30 年，很少离开，更喜欢在丛林里度过时光。他是一个超级热情友好的人，喜欢有人来拜访，这样他就可以与他们分享他对雨林的热情。当我们要出发时，正当我踏上小型飞机离开时，他从门外探出头来问我要回哪里，我说伦敦。他立即回应道：“哦，你最好小心点。你们那个市长……”我吃了一惊，说：“是啊，那他呢？”“明年你就会有伊斯兰教法，记住我的话。他对伦敦所做的事情太可怕了。无论如何，安全飞行！”我还没来得及说什么，他就离开了。令我震惊的是，一个生活在世界另一端、从未去过伦敦、生活在一个很少考虑英国、与外界接触非常有限的国家的人，竟然会对这里发生的事情抱有如此强烈和扭曲的看法。从我这几天看到的所有证据来看，他是一个善良、体贴的人，但他的大脑显然充满了种族主义、极右翼的废话。第二名是来自世界另一端偏远山区的读者：去年七月我在澳大利亚，正在格兰屏国家公园游览。当导游（一个新西兰裔澳大利亚人）过来和我坐在一起时，我正在吃午饭。我一直在问他关于鸟类的问题，但后来他问我是否可以问一个关于英国的问题，然后说“英国即将爆发内战吗？”我简直大吃一惊！他说他一直在 YouTube 上看一些东西，其中包括一个在伦敦老学校馅饼店逛的人。我有点关闭了车队，因为它太奇怪了。 另一位读者内森（Nathan）联系了阿拉伯联合酋长国的一篇报道，那里的 BBC World Service 已被埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）的 X World Service 取代：去年年底，我在迪拜，为阿布扎比的 F1 旅行，与出租车司机有过一次非常奇怪的经历。我向他提到我住在伦敦，他立即开始谈论那里有多危险以及我应该如何小心。他描述了犯罪率如何上升，人们的珠宝如何在街上被偷，以及刀具无处不在。他说话的方式让他听起来对这个地方非常熟悉，所以我猜想他最近住在伦敦或者至少访问过伦敦。直到后来他才说他从未去过伦敦或英国，他的整个印象都是基于他在社交媒体上看到的以及从他出租车里的其他人那里听到的。现在的伦敦并不完美，但考虑到我们当时行驶在反乌托邦的八车道高速公路上，烟雾遮蔽了天空的所有视野，我很高兴几天后回到伦敦。来自世界各地的轶事继续涌入我们的收件箱。有一位洛杉矶亲戚的人担心去伦敦，因为他们的朋友在看到有关购买勺子受到限制的报道后，在 TikTok 上看到首都的刀具犯罪非常严重，现在在商店购买任何餐具都需要身份证。另一位人士表示，曼彻斯特的朋友经常对他们的安全表示担忧。时代广播电台的 Fi Glover 联系上说，她的一位亲戚现在拒绝从萨里来到伦敦市中心，因为“这是一个地狱般的禁区，她肯定会被抢劫，而且所有商店都用木板封起来”。一个共同点是，人们认为首都街道上不断发生连绵不断的暴力骚乱。这并不是说，仅仅因为最近谋杀率下降的消息，伦敦的生活就一帆风顺。如果你读过《London Centric》，你就会知道我们已经爆出了一些新闻，比如某些形式的盗窃行为在伦敦实际上是合法化的，房屋里蟑螂出没，被盗的手机被丢弃，警方几乎没有任何兴趣。无论您是非法冰淇淋车、狡猾的慈善筹款人，还是用红漆涂抹房屋的黑社会团伙……我们都会追捕您。但 London Centric 之所以仔细报道这些故事并确立事实，是因为我们认为一座伟大的城市可以变得更好——我们希望向当权者施加压力，以改善我们真正喜欢居住的地方。对于一些只需要廉价点击的 YouTube 用户、X 海报或 TikTok 用户来说，伦敦只不过是一个拍摄地点，为他们提供背景镜头，无论什么病毒式的愤怒诱饵都能赚取几美元的广告收入。未来几周我们将更多地了解这些账户的幕后黑手。发表评论无论如何，伦敦人完全有能力制造自己的病毒式恐慌。周五，一段鼓励学生攻击邻近学校学生的视频开始在 TikTok 上流传，伦敦东部哈克尼的学校陷入混乱。一个自称 Hackney706 的帐户发布的剪辑将该行政区的八所学校分为“红色”或“蓝色”组，并敦促 9 年级的学生攻击与相反颜色的学校的任何学生。这些视频称之为“哈克尼战争”，鼓励学生拍摄任何攻击行为，然后将视频发送到 TikTok 帐户进行发布。病毒视频的屏幕截图，学校名称模糊。在“使用暴力”字样下，帖子列出了可能的武器，其中包括包括指南针、金属梳子和尺子。当局很难判断这些帖子是合法的暴力威胁，还是拥有 TikTok 账户的小学生的幻想。第三种选择介于两者之间——由于单个可疑帖子的迅速传播，事件可能会意外成为现实。大都会警察局告诉 London Centric，他们非常重视视频威胁，特别是在本周布伦特一所学校发生刺伤事件之后。 London Centric 的记者在哈克尼花了一天时间观察 TikTok 信息造成的大量警力，学校部署了工作人员以确保学生分散。哈克尼的一家麦当劳分店受到了极其严密的保护。警察向哈克尼的马雷街地区发出了驱散令，允许他们在 48 小时内将认为存在反社会行为风险的人员从该地区带走。麦当劳当地的哈克尼分店也不允许任何 16 岁以下的人在没有成人陪同的情况下进入，警察聚集在外面。放学时，哈克尼市中心挤满了小学生，他们看起来对没有发生任何事件感到非常失望，在周围转来转去，同时试图避免与警察目光接触。学童们似乎主要因为无法进入麦当劳而感到恼火。一名女士看到大量警察在场，询问记者“玛莎百货是否发生过抢劫”。警方和学校担心 TikTok 上的信息传播可能会成为自我实现的预言，这是可以理解的。同样清楚的是，真正的威胁和青少年在互联网上胡闹之间的界限越来越模糊且难以处理。我们一直关注伦敦最富有的居民在做什么。这就是为什么 London Centric 很感兴趣地看到一位用户名为 Gcottrell93 的在线赌徒在周一下午进行了一系列总计 9 万英镑的赌注，押注凯尔·斯塔默 (Keir Starmer) 将在 2 月底被罢黜首相职务。 GCottrell93 账户此前曾与曾居住在肯辛顿的乔治·科特雷尔 (George Cottrell) 有关，他是一名职业赌徒、被定罪的欺诈者，也是英国改革党领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇 (Nigel Farage) 的无薪助手。霍尔本和圣潘克拉斯议员被废黜的赌注看起来是一个糟糕的是——如果 Gcottrell93 在撰写本文时兑现，他们将损失 20,000 英镑。 Reform UK 没有回应置评请求，询问该账户是否真的属于伦敦最富有的赌徒之一，以及他在总部工作期间是否可以涉足政治。普通读者会知道我们对伦敦最大房东之一阿西夫·阿齐兹 (Asif Aziz) 的 Criterion Capital 的奇怪商业行为进行了庞大且看似无止尽的调查。该公司控制着皮卡迪利广场周围的大部分建筑，收购了郊区的酒吧，并购买了购物中心，例如达尔斯顿的大型重建场地。该公司容忍其最著名的地产中存在逃税礼品店，经营食品卫生标准差的山寨商标侵权餐厅，导致儿童住院，并对查尔斯王子电影院和Picturehouse Central构成生存威胁。它还拥有良好的人脉关系：阿齐兹家族通过其慈善基金会，为工党议员和主要报纸的办公室工作人员提供了报酬。幸运的是，越来越多与 Criterion 有关的消息来源对公司的商业行为表示担忧，他们有信心与 London Centric 交谈。 （如果你在那里工作，你可以匿名联系。）最近几周我们听到的最奇怪的事情之一是，经法院批准派了法警去执行 Criterion Capital 一再拒绝支付的一笔非常小的债务。据信，这场纠纷是由于该公司在其网站上使用了一张未经许可的高力木塔开发项目照片。当法警被派去执行债务时，结果在皮卡迪利广场 Zedwell 酒店外与 Criterion 员工发生了暴力争执，其中一些人受伤。伦敦警察局的一位发言人向 London Centric 证实：“1 月 21 日星期三 09:30 左右，警方被叫到皮卡迪利大风车街的一家企业，此前有报道称，两名法警和一名公司成员之间发生了争执。该处所的工作人员出席并进行了调查，没有发现任何需要进一步调查的罪行，也没有逮捕任何人。”这就提出了一个问题：当伦敦一家主要房东的员工因未能偿还小额债务而在办公室外发生争执时，这对伦敦一家主要房东的商业事务有何影响？ Criterion 没有回应置评请求。伦敦的天气灰暗而悲惨，但夏季节日季的规划已经在进行中。越来越明显的是，去年的布罗克韦尔公园之战正在给整个首都的节日发起者带来严重影响。对于那些不记得的人来说，去年一群伦敦南部人将兰贝斯市议会告上法庭，原因是布罗克韦尔公园因举办“Mighty Hoopla”等售票活动而被围起来的天数。传统上，伦敦的主办方会利用临时开发权来举办节日，并得到同一个委员会的批准，该委员会将从出租公园中获得经济利益。布罗克韦尔公园当地人表示，他们实际上费心阅读了国家规划法，并得出结论，这种标准做法是非法的。他们认为，如果节日主办方想要在公园内建造超过 28 天的建筑，包括搭建和拆除时间，他们需要申请完整的规划许可。一名高等法院法官同意当地人的观点，这让整个伦敦节日模式陷入混乱。AEG 是伦敦东部维多利亚公园活动的大型推广商，其中包括 All Points East 和 LIDO，它是重新适应新常态的公司之一——正如我们去年 5 月首次建议的那样。由于布罗克韦尔公园的裁决，它必须向陶尔哈姆莱茨议会提交一份完整的规划申请，其中表示希望在未来六年内每年夏天接管维多利亚公园的一大片土地，为期 75 天，以便举办 11 场重大节日活动和 8 场社区活动。公众将第一次有机会正式反对和审查维多利亚公园的提案，并可能向当地议员施加压力，要求做出改变。泰晤士河部分地区将正式监测野生游泳质量的消息引起了我们的注意。政府宣布将于 5 月至 9 月对特罗洛克岛附近的一段河段进行水质检查。它位于金斯顿和特丁顿船闸之间，这意味着它不会受到河流下游潮汐的影响。该地点还勉强避开了克兰河和布伦特河上未处理的污水，伦敦中心实际上已经涉过这两条河，因为成千上万的房产绕过泰晤士水务公司的下水道，将污水直接排入河流。解决伦敦的其他污水丑闻是真正的挑战如果萨迪克·汗想实现他更多地在河里游泳的愿望。在一系列备受瞩目的保守党转向改革党之后，宣布丹·托马斯担任该党威尔士领导人时遇到了一个问题：谁？由于威尔士居民纷纷在谷歌上搜索他们的新人是谁，北伦敦巴尼特邮报的流量激增。托马斯长期以来一直是北伦敦政坛的常客，在担任领导“easyCouncil”外包改革的内阁成员后，他于 2019 年至 2022 年成为巴尼特议会的保守党领袖。该计划因其廉价航空公司的启发而在媒体上赢得了绰号，该计划旨在引入一项大幅削减成本的计划，仅提供法定最低限度的内部服务，而将其他所有服务外包给服务提供商 Capita，合同价值约为 5 亿英镑。Reform 将目光投向了伦敦郊区的外围行政区，例如 Barnet，它希望在那里获得巨大收益。但托马斯去年并没有领导这场竞选，而是决定回到他长大的威尔士。尽管住在数百英里之外，他仍然担任 Finchley Church End 选区议员直到 12 月。现任巴尼特保守党领袖彼得·津金 (Peter Zinkin) 告诉 London Centric，托马斯应该退还他“长期缺席巴尼特”期间支付给他的议员津贴，此前曾批评他“放弃了该行政区、改变政党并让居民失望”。津金表示，新任威尔士改革领袖“去年的参与程度非常有限 [...]如果你的工作不在巴尼特，并且你不再住在巴尼特，那么你实际上没有资格成为一名议员。”当托马斯担任巴尼特议会领导人时，他积极地将伦敦描绘成一个多元化的城市。 2020 年乔治·弗洛伊德 (George Floyd) 被杀后，威尔士新任改革领袖表示，这些事件“让我反思国内外的种族主义，以及我为代表巴尼特感到多么自豪，巴尼特区因其丰富的多样性而变得更好、更有趣。”这篇文章由伦敦中心与威尔·海沃德威尔士时事通讯联合报道，对于任何想要了解威尔士政治的人来说，这都是一本很好的读物。读者密切关注伦敦环境和交通裁决员的年度报告，这些裁决员负责处理交通违法行为的上诉。对于那些还没有意识到的人来说，很明显人工智能正在造成越来越多的混乱。人们要求聊天机器人写下他们的诉求，而人工智能系统热衷于取悦用户，然后写出毫无法律依据的毫无根据的胡言乱语，然后必须由法庭正式考虑。“审判人员发现，这些‘幻觉’案件出现在他们面前的辩论中，”交通审判人员说。 “在一次上诉中，上诉人引用的 7 个案例中有 5 个不存在。另外两个是真实案例，但它们与上诉人提出的观点不符。”我们不断听到同样的事情——人工智能生成的法律上诉正在堵塞伦敦的议会、法院和公共服务部门。过去用绿色墨水抱怨的人们现在可以提交一页又一页听起来合法的人工智能废话，这些废话在法律上必须由权威人士考虑。如果您有任何好的例子，请与我们联系。我们将于下周回来进行更多原创调查。感谢所有为 London Centric 付费并使这一切成为可能的人。如果您喜欢这个版本，请将其转发给朋友并传播出去。发表评论

Original Summary

  1. London Centric tends to publish twice a week, to avoid clogging up your inbox. Sometimes we bring you a single investigation that’s taken months of work. Sometimes we sprinkle some nuggets of news on top, or updates on ongoing stories. And sometimes, like today, we want to bring you a little bit of everything. Please enjoy this weekend update, which is full of exclusive stories. Promise there’ll be something you haven’t read elsewhere as you scroll down – or your money back. Fresh investigations are coming next week.Got a story we should be looking into? Get in touch over email or WhatsApp.Thanks to all the readers who got in touch to share their stories of encountering people in some of the most remote corners of the world (which I define as meaning locations ranging from Suriname to Surrey) who think London is in a state of total societal collapse.This follows our story about the would-be TikTok entrepreneur who said he decided to specialise in fake videos about London homes being given to illegal immigrants because “hate brings views”. On Friday night the Met Police told us they are continuing to investigate whether an offence was committed by the individual behind the TikTok account, who wrongly thought they were posting without any potential consequences.It’s increasingly clear that London is suffering a global reputational crisis exacerbated by the incentives of viral video sites. It’s impacting tourism, investment in the capital, and the general sense of wellbeing in the city. It’s driven by legitimate raw videos that feature highly visible thefts of mobile phones and violent robberies. It’s then exaggerated by unscrupulous content creators and social media platforms who incentivise ragebait about the supposed total collapse of the city’s society.First place for the most remote encounter went to London Centric reader Joe Jones:In November 2025, I was visiting Suriname on holiday. We flew in this tiny plane to a remote jungle outpost in the Amazon rainforest, where we stayed for five days. It is completely off the grid with this small eight-seater plane coming and going twice a week to drop off/pick up guests and drop off supplies. And there was no access to the internet for the guests staying there.It was run by this Surinamese bloke of about 60. He’d worked there for about 30 years and would rarely leave, preferring to spend his time in the jungle. He was a super warm and friendly guy, and loved having people visiting so he could share his passion for the rainforest with them.When it came time for us to go, just as I was stepping into the small plane to leave, he poked his head through the door and asked where I was going back to, and I said London. He instantly responded, “oh you better watch out. That mayor of yours…” I was taken aback and said, “Yeah, what about him?”“You’ll have Sharia Law by next year, mark my words. It’s terrible what he’s done to London. Anyway, fly safe!” and he left before I could say anything.It was really striking to me that a man on the other side of the world, who’d never been to London, living in a country that rarely even thinks about the UK, and with very limited exposure to the outside world, could have such a strong and distorted opinion about what’s happening here. A man who from all the evidence I had seen those few days was kind and thoughtful, but who had clearly had his brain pumped full of racist, far-right crap.Second place went to the reader who was in remote mountains on the other side of the world:I was in Australia back in July last year, and was on a tour of Grampians National Park. Was sat having lunch when the guide, a Kiwi-Aussie guy, came to sit with me. I’d been asking him about the birds but then he asked me if he could ask a question about the UK, and then came out with “so is the UK about to have a civil war?”I was just flabbergasted! He said he’d been watching stuff on YouTube, including a guy who goes round old school pie shops in London. I kind of shut the convo down as it was so weird.Nathan, another reader, got in touch with a report from the United Arab Emirates, where the BBC World Service has been replaced with Elon Musk’s X world service: I was in Dubai at the end of last year, travelling for the F1 in Abu Dhabi and had a very odd experience with a taxi driver. I mentioned to him that I lived in London, and he immediately started talking about how dangerous it was and how I ought to be careful. He described how crime had been going up, how people were getting jewellery stolen off them in the street and that knives were everywhere. The way he talked made him sound very familiar with the place so I assumed he had lived in or at least visited London recently. Only later did he say that he’d never been to London or the UK and that his entire impression was based on what he’d seen on social media and heard from other people in his cab.Now London’s not perfect, but given the dystopian eight-lane motorway we were driving on at the time and that the smog was obscuring all view of the sky, I was quite glad to be heading back to London a few days later.The anecdotes from around the world continue to flood into our inbox. There was the person with the relative in Los Angeles who was worried about going to London because their friend had seen a TikTok claiming that knife crime in the capital is so bad you now need ID to buy any cutlery from a shop, after seeing stories about restrictions on buying spoons. Another person said friends in Manchester regularly raised concerns about their safety. Times Radio’s Fi Glover got in touch to say one of her relatives now refused to come in from Surrey to central London because “it’s a hellish no go area, she’d be mugged for sure and all the shops were boarded up anyway”. A common thread was the perception that there are constant, rolling violent riots on the capital’s streets.This isn’t about saying that life in London is all fine and dandy just because of the recent news that the murder rate is in decline. If you’ve read London Centric you’ll know we’ve broken news on how certain forms of theft are effectively legalised in the capital, housing can be infested with cockroaches, and stolen mobile phones are discarded with barely any police interest. Whether you’re an illegal ice cream van, a dodgy charity fundraiser, or a triad gang daubing houses in red paint… we’re still coming after you. But the reason London Centric carefully reports these stories and establishes facts is because we think a great city can be made even better – and we want to put pressure on those in power to improve a place we actually like to live in. For some YouTubers, X posters, or TikTokers who just need cheap clicks, London is nothing more than a filming location that provides them with the background footage for whatever viral ragebait will earn a few dollars in advertising revenue. We’ll have more on who’s behind these accounts in the coming weeks.Leave a commentIn any case, Londoners are more than capable of creating their own viral panics.Schools in Hackney, east London, were thrown into chaos on Friday when a video began circulating on TikTok encouraging students to attack pupils at neighbouring schools. Clips posted by an account calling itself Hackney706 divided eight of the borough’s schools into either “red” or “blue” groups and urged Year 9 students to assault any student from a school with the opposing colour.Calling it a “Hackney War,” the videos encourage pupils to film any attacks, before sending the footage to the TikTok account to be posted.Screengrabs of the viral video, with the school names blurred.Under the words “be violent,” the post lists possible weapons, which include a compass, metal comb, and rulers. What’s hard for the authorities to tell is whether such posts are a legitimate threat of violence or the fantasy of a single school pupil with a TikTok account. A third option is somewhere in between – an incident could accidentally become reality thanks to the rapid spread of a single dubious post.The Met Police told London Centric they took the video threats very seriously, especially after this week’s stabbing at a school in Brent. London Centric’s reporter spent the day in Hackney observing the heavy police presence caused by the TikTok message, as schools deployed staff in a bid to ensure pupils dispersed.An incredibly well-protected branch of McDonald’s in Hackney.Officers issued a dispersal order to the Mare Street area of Hackney, allowing them to remove individuals from the area for 48 hours if they believe there’s a risk of antisocial behaviour. The local Hackney branch of McDonald’s was also not allowed to allow any under 16s to enter without an adult, with officers congregating outside. At school kicking-out time central Hackney filled with schoolchildren who looked very disappointed by the lack of an incident, milling around while trying to avoid eye contact with the police. The schoolchildren mainly seemed annoyed they couldn’t get into McDonald’s. One woman, eyeing up the heavy police presence, asked our reporter if “there’d been a heist at M&S”. The police and schools are understandably concerned that the virality of the messages on TikTok could become a self-fulfilling prophecy. What’s also clear is that the line between a real threat and teenagers messing about on the internet is increasingly blurry and difficult to deal with. We’re always keeping an eye on what London’s richest residents are up to. Which was why London Centric was intrigued to see an online gambler under the username GCottrell93 place a series of bets on Monday afternoon totalling £90,000 that Keir Starmer would be deposed as prime minister by the end of February. The GCottrell93 account has previously been linked to sometime Kensington resident George Cottrell, a professional gambler, convicted fraudster, and unpaid aide to Reform UK leader Nigel Farage. The bet on the Holborn and St Pancras MP being deposed looks to be a duff one – if GCottrell93 cashes out at the time of writing, they’ll lose £20,000. Reform UK did not respond to a request for comment on whether the account really is owned by one of London’s wealthiest gamblers – and whether he’s allowed to flutter on politics while working at HQ.Regular readers will know our sprawling and seemingly endless investigations into the curious business practices of Asif Aziz’s Criterion Capital, one of London’s biggest landlords. The company controls a large chunk of the buildings around Piccadilly Circus, snaps up suburban pubs, and purchases shopping centres such as the giant redevelopment site in Dalston. The company has tolerated tax-evading gift shops in its most prominent properties, run knock-off trademark-infringing restaurants with poor food hygiene standards that left children hospitalised, and posed an existential threat to both the Prince Charles Cinema and Picturehouse Central. It’s also well-connected: Through its charitable foundation, the Aziz family has paid for staff to work in the offices of Labour MPs and leading newspapers.Luckily, an ever-growing number of sources connected to Criterion, who have concerns about the company’s business practices, are gaining the confidence to speak to London Centric. (If you work there, you can get in touch anonymously.) One of the most curious things we’ve been told in recent weeks is that bailiffs were sent with court approval to enforce a very small debt that Criterion Capital had repeatedly refused to pay. The dispute is believed to be over the company’s use of an unlicensed photograph on its website of the Colliers Wood tower development. When bailiffs were sent to enforce the debt, it resulted in a violent altercation with Criterion employees outside the company's office at the Zedwell Hotel in Piccadilly Circus, leaving some of those involved with injuries.A Met Police spokesperson confirmed to London Centric: “Police were called to a business in Great Windmill Street, Piccadilly, at around 09:30hrs on Wednesday, 21 January, following reports of an altercation between two bailiffs and a member of staff at the premises. Officers attended and made enquiries. There were no offences disclosed that required further investigation and no arrests made.” Which raises the question: What does it say about the business affairs of one of London’s leading landlords when its staff are involved in fights outside their offices over their failure to pay small debts? Criterion did not respond to a request for comment.It’s grey and miserable in London – but planning for the summer festival season is already underway. It’s becoming increasingly apparent that last year’s Battle for Brockwell Park is causing serious repercussions for festival promoters across the capital. For those who can’t remember, last year a group of south Londoners took Lambeth council to court over the number of days that Brockwell Park was fenced off for ticketed events such as Mighty Hoopla. Traditionally, London promoters have used temporary development rights to host festivals, with approval nodded through by the same council that stands to benefit financially from renting out their public park. The Brockwell Park locals said they’d actually bothered to read national planning law and concluded this standard practice was illegal. They argued if a festival promoter wants to put structures on a public park for more than 28 days, including set-up and deconstruction time, they need to apply for full planning permission. A high court judge agreed with the locals, throwing the entire model of London festivals in chaos.AEG, the mega promoter of events in east London’s Victoria Park including All Points East and LIDO, is among those readjusting to the new normal — as we first suggested would happen last May. Because of the Brockwell Park ruling it is having to submit a full planning application to Tower Hamlets council, in which it states it wants to take over a large chunk of Victoria Park for 75 days every summer for the next six years, in order to host 11 major festival events plus eight community events. For the first time, the public will have a chance to formally object and scrutinise the proposals in Victoria Park – and potentially put pressure on local councillors to demand changes.News that part of the River Thames will be formally monitored for wild swimming quality has caught our eye. The government has announced that a stretch of the river by Trowlock Island will be checked for water quality between May and September. It’s between Kingston and Teddington Lock, meaning it won’t be affected by the tides on the lower reaches of the river. The location also narrowly avoids the untreated sewage from misconnected properties on the River Crane and River Brent that London Centric has literally waded through, as thousands of properties bypass Thames Water’s sewers and deposit effluent directly into the river. Solving London’s other sewage scandal is the real challenge if Sadiq Khan wants to deliver on his desire for more swimming in the river.After a series of high-profile Conservatives jumping ship to Reform, the announcement of Dan Thomas as the party’s Welsh leader was met with one question: Who? Traffic to north London’s Barnet Post spiked, as Welsh residents rushed to Google who their new man was.Thomas has long been a fixture of the north London political scene, becoming the Conservative leader of Barnet council from 2019 to 2022 after previously being the cabinet member who led its “easyCouncil” outsourcing reforms. The plan, which earned it the nickname in the press inspired by the cut-price airline, was to introduce a drastic cost-cutting plan to deliver only the legal minimum of services in-house while outsourcing everything else to service provider Capita on contracts worth around £500m.Reform has its eyes trained on London’s suburban outer boroughs, like Barnet, where it hopes to make big gains. But rather than lead that campaign, Thomas last year decided to head back to Wales, where he grew up. Despite living hundreds of miles away, he remained a councillor for Finchley Church End ward until December.Peter Zinkin, the current Barnet Conservative leader, told London Centric that Thomas should refund the councillor’s allowance paid to him during his “prolonged absence from Barnet” having previously criticised him for having “abandoned the borough, changed parties and let down residents”.Zinkin said that the new Welsh Reform leader’s “level of engagement in the last year was very modest [...] If your job is not in Barnet and you don’t live in Barnet anymore, then you don’t actually qualify to be a councillor.”When he was leader of Barnet council, Thomas pushed a positive portrayal of London as a diverse city. Following the killing of George Floyd in 2020, Wales’ new Reform leader said the events had “led me to reflect on racism at home, as well as abroad, and how proud I am to represent Barnet – a borough made so much better and more interesting thanks to its rich diversity.”This item was reported by London Centric in conjunction with the Will Hayward newsletter in Wales, which is a great read for anyone wanting to get to understand Welsh politics.We’re sure that all London Centric readers pay close attention to the annual report of London’s Environment and Traffic Adjudicators, the people who handle appeals for traffic offences. For those that haven’t, it’s clear that AI is increasingly causing chaos. People are asking chatbots to write their appeals and the AI systems, keen to please their users, are then writing absolute nonsense with no basis in legal reality which then has to be formally considered by the tribunal.“Adjudicators have found that these ‘hallucinated’ cases are appearing in arguments before them,” said the traffic adjudicator. “In one appeal, 5 of 7 cases cited by an Appellant did not exist. The other two were genuine cases but they were not on the points advanced by the Appellant.”We keep hearing the same thing – AI-generated legal appeals are clogging up London’s councils, courts, and public services. People who used to complain in green ink can now file pages upon pages of legitimate-sounding AI nonsense that legally has to be considered by those in authority. If you have any good examples of this, get in touch.We’ll be back next week with more original investigations. Thanks to all who pay for London Centric and make it possible. If you enjoyed this edition, please forward it to a friend and spread the word.Leave a comment

本书并未具体涉及英国，但它确实详细介绍了俄罗斯宣传反动媒体叙事以破坏美国和欧盟稳定的广为人知的战略。我觉得在某些时候，人们需要开始认真对待这一问题，而不是继续表现得像人们突然开始无缘无故地在互联网上讲述疯狂的种族主义谎言。 https://icct.nl/publication/russia-and-far-right-insights-te...
This book doesn’t cover the UK specifically, but it does detail Russia’s well-publicized strategy of promoting reactionary media narratives to destabilize the United States and the EU. I feel like at some point, people will need to start taking this seriously, instead of continuing to act like people suddenly just started telling insane racist lies on the internet for no reason. https://icct.nl/publication/russia-and-far-right-insights-te...

马斯克是俄罗斯特工吗？传统基金会？狐狸？特朗普2.0背后有庞大的、全美国的基础设施。

I'm sure plenty of countries did their very best to get that guy in the Whitehouse, but I don't buy that Putin is the main character here.

在 HN 中查看
carlosjobim 2026/04/07 04:04
阳光是最好的消毒剂。当你开始隐藏自己的问题并指责那些揭露这些问题的人时，你就会陷入螺旋式下降。

如果你的敌人也想推广这一点，那也没关系。如果你遵循这个思路，你就必须支持中国政府审查天安门广场大屠杀。因为他们在西方的敌人几十年来一直在谈论这个问题。

Sunshine is the best disinfectant. When you start hiding your problems and blaming those who expose them, you are on a spiral which only goes down.

It doesn't matter if your enemy also wants to promote this. If you follow this line of thought, you have to support the government of China censoring the Tiananmen Square massacre. Because their enemies in the West have for decades been talking about it.

在 HN 中查看
mbgerring 2026/04/07 04:45
我们谈论的是人们胡编乱造的事情，比如断言海地移民在郊区偷窃并吃掉美国白人的狗，等等。在我住在这里的整个时间里，旧金山也一直是这种宣传的目标。

我同意应该揭露社会问题。其中一个社会问题是普遍存在的、完全捏造的犯罪和暴力故事，这些故事被不住在所谓受影响地区的人们不加批判地接受，从而引发分裂和政治言论，导致现实世界受到伤害。

有大量证据表明，这种主要通过社交媒体传播的宣传是外国政府干预“敌对”国家内部政治战略的产物。如果这是真的，就应该曝光并关闭。

We’re talking about people making things up from whole cloth, such as asserting that Haitian immigrants steal and eat dogs from white Americans in the suburbs, among many, many such examples. San Francisco has also been a target of this kind of propaganda for the entire time I’ve lived here.

I agree that social problems should be exposed. One such social problem is widespread, entirely fabricated stories of crime and violence, accepted uncritically by people who don’t live in the supposedly affected areas, driving division and political discourse that lead to real-world harm.

There is an enormous amount of evidence that this propaganda, mainly spread through social media, is a product of a foreign government strategy to interfere in the internal politics of “enemy” nations. If this is true, it should be exposed and shut down.

mothballed 2026/04/07 04:58
我认为某些文化喜欢吃狗或其他“宠物”或不寻常的动物，这不是什么秘密。 我想知道特朗普是否将海地人和其他一些群体混为一谈。

我有很多第三世界的家庭，当我带他们去动物园时，他们花了大约一半的时间盯着他们吃过或想吃的所有动物。 当我回到他们的祖国时，我看到狗被放在明火上烤。 我个人曾不止一次地被美国的一个普通移民团体提供过一只我亲眼目睹的烤狗。

I don't think it's any secret that some cultures love to eat dogs or other "pets" or unusual animals. I wonder if Trump was mixing up Haitians with some other groups.

I have a lot of 3rd world family, when I take them to the zoo they spend about half the time ogling over all the animals they've eaten or would like to eat. When I go back to their home country, I see dogs being roasted over an open fire. I have personally been offered a dog I've witnessed roasting, more than once, by a common immigrant group in America.

fhdkweig 2026/04/07 05:19

jfengel 2026/04/07 05:15
我不清楚阳光是否曾对任何东西进行消毒。谎言比真相传播得更快。

这并不是说隐藏问题会有任何好处。只是暴露它们也解决不了任何问题。你确实必须解决自己的问题，但你也需要通过让人们停止撒谎来获得一些喘息的空间。

It is unclear to me that sunlight has ever disinfected anything. Lies spread faster than the truth.

That's not to say you'll do any good by hiding problems. It's just that exposing them isn't going to fix anything, either. You do have to fix your own problems, but you also need to get some breathing room by getting people to cease lying about it.

bragr 2026/04/07 04:37
>有个亲戚在洛杉矶，担心去伦敦

这让我很有趣，因为住在洛杉矶/加利福尼亚州，我得到了同样风格的精神错乱的评论，完全脱离了这里发生的现实。我们当然也有自己的问题，但你可能会认为我们是索马里，正如某些人所说的那样。去年夏天，我有一位来自阿肯色州的朋友来访，这确实改变了他的世界观。

我认为这只是偏转。从政治上来说，谈论加州的糟糕道路或无家可归者问题比谈论你所在州的学校在全国排名后 20% 更方便。

>There was the person with the relative in Los Angeles who was worried about going to London

This amused me because living in LA/California, I get the same style unhinged comments completely disconnected from the reality of what is happening here. We certainly have our share of problems, but you'd think we're Somalia the way certain people talk. I had a friend from Arkansas visit last summer and it definitely reset his world view a bit.

I assume this is all just deflection. It is more politically convenient to talk about bad roads or the homeless problem in California, than to address that your state's schools are in the bottom 20% of the nation.

alsetmusic 2026/04/07 04:01
安妮·凯利 (Annie Kelly) 最近主持了一期关于这一现象的 QAA 播客。人们来到伦敦并拍摄自己与周围“可怕”的人一起行走的视频。这主要是一堆恐惧诱饵的垃圾。

Annie Kelly recently hosted an episode of the QAA podcast about this exact phenomenon. People come to London and video themselves walking around with “scary” people around them. It’s mostly a bunch of fear-bait garbage.

The episode is titled “London Has Fallen (E356)” and was released on Jan 26 of this year. Worth a listen.

cedws 2026/04/07 04:21
不，这有很多道理。抢夺和抢劫电话很常见，但报道较少，因为从未发生过任何事情。我去过很多地方，除了布鲁塞尔之外，伦敦（我的家乡）是唯一一个我畏缩并保护我的手机以免它被抢走的地方。我不能让我的包或笔记本电脑离开我的视线，哪怕一秒钟。

轻微犯罪通过侵蚀信任来削弱社会，需要以新加坡式的方式进行惩罚。

Nah, there's a lot of truth to it. Phone snatching and looting is common but underreported because nothing ever happens. I've travelled quite a lot and other than Brussels, London (my home city) is the only one where I'm cowering and shielding my phone for fear it will be snatched. I can't leave my bag or laptop out of sight for even a second.

Petty crime chips away at society by eroding trust, it needs to be punished Singapore style.

在 HN 中查看
rcxdude 2026/04/07 06:15
通常，你不会通过更严厉的惩罚来阻止此类犯罪，而是通过更严厉的惩罚来阻止它们 始终如一地，这是一个更昂贵的解决方案。
You don't generally stop such crimes by punishing them more harshly, you stop them by punishing them more consistently, which is a more expensive solution.

jzellis 2026/04/07 04:05
我是美国人，住在伦敦郊外的沃特福德，乘坐火车 17 分钟即可到达尤斯顿车站。我走遍了整个城市，从未感到不安全或紧张，甚至不需要特别警惕。当人们说这里存在“枪支犯罪”时，他们的意思是每隔几个月就会有人开枪并成为全国新闻。我在美国西部各地都住过，那里甚至比我住过的乡村小镇都安全。所有这些东西都被夸张得可笑了，当其他英国人谈论伦敦有多么危险时，请记住，这就像农夫吉尔斯谈论霍比屯太大，充满了说话有趣的胡言乱语。对于美国人来说这很搞笑。就像拿着开箱刀的孩子一样。对于任何曾经在洛杉矶这样的美国城市生活过的人来说，这确实是夏尔。这都是法拉吉瓦尔德·莫斯利和他的特姆靴子们炮制的右翼宣传。

对于那些把自己的全部个性都归因于他们的祖父在战争中所做的事情的人来说，他们肯定会表现得像他对那些人所做的那样。

I'm an American who lives just outside London in Watford, and I'm 17 minutes from Euston Station by train. I have been all over the city and never felt unsafe or nervous or even the need to be especially vigilant. When people say there's "gun crime" here they mean that once every few months someone fires a gun and it makes the national news. I've lived all over the American West and it's safer than even the rural towns I've lived in. All of this stuff is hilariously overblown, and when other British people talk about how dangerous London is, keep in mind it's like Farmer Giles talking about how Hobbiton is too big and full of futtiners who talk funny. To an American it's hilarious. It's, like, kids with boxcutters. For anyone who's ever lived in an American city like LA, this really is the Shire. It's all right-wing propaganda cooked up by Faragewald Mosley and his Temu bootboys.

For people who make their entire personalities about what their grandpa did in the War, they're sure seem hellbent on acting exactly like the people he did it to.

themaninthedark 2026/04/07 04:42
我不会说纽约是一个地狱，但会说他们（当地人）似乎并没有那么严重地对待那里的犯罪，甚至是暴力犯罪。

去年秋天我和家人一起去参观，乘渡轮去雕像时把车停在新泽西州一侧。他们坐火车到酒店，我去取车，在前台看到一个人在购物中心用链子殴打保安。

盖伊在车上撒尿，警卫叫他停下来。他对此很生气，拿起一根链子开始踢门，这样警卫就会叫他停下来。他一出来，那人就用链子打他的头。

警方花了 15 到 20 分钟才做出反应，似乎对寻找该男子不感兴趣。警卫对提出指控不感兴趣。

盖伊可能无家可归，肯定需要心理健康，但他有能力策划并执行可能致命的暴力袭击。

I wouldn't say NYC is a hell hole but will say they(locals) don't seem to take crime that serious there, even violent crime.

I was visiting last fall with the family, left the car in the NJ side when taking the ferry to the Statue. They took the train to the hotel and I went to retrieve the car, got a front stage view of a guy using a chain to beat up a security guard at a shopping mall.

Guy had been peeing on the vehicles, guard told him to stop. He took offense at this, got a length of chain and started kicking the door so the guard would tell him to stop. As soon as he came out guy started hitting him over the head with the chain.

Police took a good 15 to 20 minutes to respond, didn't seem interested in looking for the guy. The guard wasn't interested in pressing charges.

Guy was probably homeless and definitely needed mental health but he had the capacity to plan out and execute a violent attack that could have been deadly.

tracker1 2026/04/07 04:02

在 HN 中查看
defrost 2026/04/07 04:25
监管，而不是禁止，不是工作刀（厨师、绳工、技术人员，<原因>），而是“僵尸”刀和其他用于在公共场合挥舞和恐吓的“闪光”刀（受特定性能原因等影响）

喜欢它，讨厌它，这与美国的做法不同，最终取决于法官以普通公民在公共汽车上的“合理行为”作为衡量标准。

Regulate, not ban, not working knives (chef, ropemaker, tech, <reason>), but "zombie" knives and other "flash" used to swing in public and intimidate (subject, say to specific performance reasons, etc.)

Love it, hate it, it's a different mindset to the US approach and ultimatelty falls back on judges using "reasonable behaviour" of common citizens on ominbuses as a yardstick.

在 HN 中查看
defrost 2026/04/07 06:51
呵呵，很棒的赛道。我的意思是，这不是林顿·奎西·约翰逊的言论，但它就是这样，这就足够了。

听着，没有人喜欢村里的白痴在拥挤的购物中心骑着独轮车玩弄点燃的炸药，如果坏苹果*没有得到更好的方法去做，这对任何人都没有好处，所以罗马法国家往往有任何旧的借口法，让人们有理由质疑他们为什么要做他们正在做的事情……我的祖母总是这样拉起孩子们。

这些事情的好处是 实际上 有问题和可疑的行为可以被转移到一个方向，任何肤色、语言、行政区地址的厨师都可以带着他们的刀卷自豪而自由地行走。

缺点是观察者和监护人在选择项圈时可能会变得有点热情和选择性，他们可能会发展出怪异和腐败的小集团，而挑剔的类型可能会对所有错误的事情变得有点过于挑剔。

任何社区面临的挑战是处理所有这些问题并更好地控制系统..需要时间和注意力，“很容易污染”。

* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-MVsBS7OZvo

Heh, great track. I mean it's no Linton Kwesi Johnson dis but it is what it is and that's enough.

Look, no one's a fan of the village idiot juggling lit dynamite on a unicycle in the packed shopping mall, and it's no good for anyone if the bad apples* aren't given a route to better things to do so Roman Law countries tend to have any old excuse laws to give cause to have people questioned as to why they're doing whatever the heck it is that they're doing .. my grandmother pulled up kids all the time like that.

The upside of such things is actually problematic and questionable bahaviour can be shunted in one direction and chefs of any colour, langauge, borough address can walk on proud and free with their knife rolls.

The downside is the watchers and guardians can get a bit enthused and selective in their choices of collar, they can develop little clique's of weirdness and corruption, and the judgy types can get a bit overly judgy about all the wrong things.

The challenge for any community is dealing with all that and having better control over the system .. takes time and focus, 'taint easy.

* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-MVsBS7OZvo

rcxdude 2026/04/07 06:20
一般来说，英国的暴力犯罪（至少）更多地取决于你是谁。针对旁观者的随机暴力行为非常罕见，绝大多数是受害者认识的人发起的袭击，通常与帮派有关。
Violent crimes in general in the UK (at least) are more localised to who you are. Random acts of violence on bystanders are very rare, the vast majority are attacks by someone known to the victim, often gang related.

turkey99 2026/04/07 03:52

确实没有任何位置。它的社会经济。 16-24 岁的年轻黑人男性死于持刀犯罪的比例过高。

在 HN 中查看
sourcegrift 2026/04/07 03:54
“社会经济”背后的根源是什么？
What are the root causes behind "socio-economic"?
在 HN 中查看
geysersam 2026/04/07 05:00
有许多。它是一系列往往与贫困和社会问题相关的情况的总称。

对社会的信任度低，改善经济状况的机会很少，创伤和创伤后应激障碍的患病率较高，滥用药物的可能性较高，入狱的机会成本较低，好的榜样较少，自尊心较差，教育成果较差，身体健康状况较差，参与有组织犯罪的可能性较高，依赖平行社会结构获得安全和保护的可能性较高等。

每一个都可以是自我强化网络中的原因或结果。找出一个根本原因实际上是不可能的。

There are many. It's an umbrella term for a range of circumstances that tend to be correlated with poverty and social issues.

Low trust in society, few opportunities to improve economic situation, higher prevalence of trauma and ptsd, higher probability of substance abuse, low opportunity cost for going to jail, fewer good role models, worse self esteem, worse education outcomes, worse physical health, higher likelihood of being involved in organized crime, higher likelihood of depending on parallel social structures for safety and protection, etc.

Each can be cause or effect in a self reinforcing network. Picking one single root cause isn't really possible.

在 HN 中查看
mothballed 2026/04/07 04:02
其中之一似乎是文化。

例如，我在火车上陷入持刀打斗的境地，因为在某些文化中，大声播放另一种文化的音乐是不可接受的。 一名西班牙裔男子在火车上大声播放西班牙音乐，当火车进入黑人社区时，一名黑人告诉他这是“他的头巾”并要求他停下来，随后双方都拔出了刀子。

我现在了解到，在某些社会经济飞地，文化规定我不能大声播放音乐，否则我会被刺死。

One seems to be culture.

For instance, I was caught between a knife fight on a train, because in one hood some of the culture is it's unacceptable to play another culture's music too loud. A Hispanic guy was playing hispanic music quite loud on the train, as soon as it entered a black neighborhood a black guy informed him it was "his hood" and asked him to stop, which then escalated to both pulling out knives.

I have now learned there are certain socio-economic enclaves where culture has dictated that I must not play my music too loud or I will be stabbed to death.

jeffbee 2026/04/07 04:05

mothballed 2026/04/07 04:09
而且没有任何车辆问题，因为你通常只是穿过不好的区域才能到达更好的区域。 大多数生活在暴力城市的人没有理由在暴力地区停留。 有一次，我被迫在城里一个糟糕的地方为医院的重要手术工作通宵工作，在回来的路上，我的轮胎漏气了，当我分心修理轮胎时，当地人注意到我很虚弱，他们用枪指着我的头。
And not having any vehicle problems, because you usually only are rolling through bad areas to get to better areas. Most people in violent cities have no occasion to stop in violent areas. On one occasion I was forced to work overnight for critical hospital operations in a bad part of town, on my way back my tire went flat and when I was distracted fixing it the locals noticed I was weak and they put a gun to my head.

rob_c 2026/04/07 03:50
从伦敦起飞的航班正在起飞。这也已经在很大程度上发生了。

与其他英国城市相比（以及您看到的任何过渡性地区标识），您在那里再也看不到任何伦敦风格的东西了。

伦敦的凶杀案有所下降，但这并不是 100%，因为这样更安全。大量的内容都集中在死亡而不是攻击上，所以不要欺骗自己，因为他们没有死，所以什么也没有发生。

> 犯罪在英国不是问题。

没有。甚至不接近真实。是的，我们没有校园枪击案。是的，我们没有人用装满子弹的桶来伸张“正义”。但我们确实存在枪支犯罪，并且枪支被大量用作恐吓手段。我希望我不是在一个我知道这是真的的地区长大的。

试图假装没有问题是很棒的。在这种情况下，我可以向您推荐一些价格非常合理的区域，这些区域必须非常安全，并且根本不存在社会凝聚力问题，无论您来自哪里......

Flight from London is happening. It's already happened in a large way as well.

You don't see cockney anything anywhere there anymore compared to (and as much as you see any transitional regional identify left in) other British cities.

Homicide is on the drop in London but that's not 100% because it's safer. A huge amount is focused on deaths rather than attacks so don't fool yourself that just because they didn't die that nothing happened.

> crime which is a non-issue in the UK.

Nope. Not even close to true. Yes we don't have school shooters. Yes we don't have people exacting "justice" with a loaded barrel. But we do have gun crime and guns are used a LOT as intimidation. I wish I didn't grow up in an area where I know that to be true.

Trying to pretend there's not a problem is wonderful. And in that case I can point you to some very reasonably priced areas which must be perfectly safe and have no social cohesion issues at all regardless of where you're from...

在 HN 中查看
alexriddle 2026/04/07 03:59
入院人数是暴力犯罪和刺伤事件的可靠指标——如果你受伤了，你需要去看医生，他们会记录下来——而且这些指标正在下降[1]。几乎没有迹象表明正在发生任何形式的流行病或暴力犯罪的增加，而且这方面的统计数据似乎也得到了证实。

更严重的问题是反社会犯罪增多，例如街头抢劫或入店行窃。这些犯罪更有可能是抢夺和抢夺，不涉及暴力。它们仍然对受害者产生影响，但并没有让这座城市变得更加暴力。

[1] https://www.london.gov.uk/london-records-fewest-homicides-ye...

Hospital admissions are reliable indicators for violent crime and stabbings in particular - if you get injured you're going to need a doctor and they will record it - and these are going down [1]. There is little to suggest any kind of epidemic or increase in violent crime is going on and the stats on this seem to play out.

What is more of an issue is more antisocial crime such as street robbery or shoplifting. These crimes are much more likely to be snatch and grab, with no violence involved. They still have an impact on the victims but they're not making the city significantly more violent.

[1] https://www.london.gov.uk/london-records-fewest-homicides-ye...

lazyasciiart 2026/04/07 03:58
> 正在从伦敦起飞的航班。这也已经在很大程度上发生了

我认识的人中没有一个是因为犯罪或安全而搬出伦敦的。他们几乎总是搬家，因为他们可以支付城市中一个小地方的费用，单程通勤时间超过一个小时，或者他们可以支付相同的费用在城外的一个较大的地方，并在火车上通勤相同的时间。

> Flight from London is happening. It's already happened in a large way as well

None of the people I've known who moved out of London did so because of crime or safety. They almost invariably moved because they could pay for a tiny place in the city and commute for over an hour each way or they could pay the same for a larger place outside the city and commute the same length of time on the train.

jeffbee 2026/04/07 03:48
全球有一个巨大的行业，告诉人们他们从未去过的遥远地方是糟糕和危险的。这个行业的存在是为了让人们忽视他们实际居住的客观上恶劣和危险的地方。这在全球和国家范围内发挥着作用。例如，我住在俄克拉荷马城的家人经常大喊大叫，谈论他们在旧金山看到的一些恐吓言论，而他们的城市的凶杀率是旧金山的四倍，交通暴力、贫困、药物滥用，甚至 人为地震。但他们却每天 7 天、每天 24 小时不断地传播虚假信息。
There's a gigantic global industry of telling people that a far away place they've never been is bad and dangerous. This industry exists to make people overlook the objectively bad and dangerous place where they actually live. This plays out on global and national scale. For example, my family who live in Oklahoma City are constantly yapping about some fearmongering they saw about San Francisco, when their city has like 4x the homicide rate, traffic violence, poverty, substance abuse, even manmade earthquakes. But they are plugging into a 24x7 stream of disinformation.
在 HN 中查看
dylan604 2026/04/07 04:25
如果您访问当地城市以外的某个地方，您可能会体验到更广泛的人民、文化和信仰。这会让你成为一个危险的人。您可能会开始质疑为什么只被告知一件事。遗憾的是，仅在美国境内旅行就可以让您接触到一些东西，但这主要只是同一件事的变体，也许还吸收了一些外部影响。这与出国体验完全不同的文化没什么不同。

关于这些外国土地的可怕社交媒体视频只是阻止人们想要扩展他们的经历的另一个工具。如果你研究一下这个全球性的行业，你会发现很多有影响力的人都是来自宣传来源的中心枢纽的辐条，而令人震惊的是，这些来源是由同一个人资助的。

If you visit some place outside your local city, you might experience a broader swath of people, culture, and beliefs. That makes you a dangerous person. You might start questioning why you've only been told one thing. Sadly, just traveling within the US can get you some exposure, but it's mainly just variations of the same thing with perhaps some external influences that have been absorbed. It's nothing like going to abroad and experiencing totally different cultures.

Scary social media videos about those foreign lands is just another tool to dissuade people from wanting to expand their experiences. If you research into some of this global industry, you'll see a lot of the influencers are spokes from central hubs of propaganda sources that shockingly are financed by the same people.

jerlam 2026/04/07 05:54
这不仅限于遥远的地方。有很多有影响力的人和社交媒体骗子专门鹦鹉学舌地向居住在这些城市的人们宣传城市有多么糟糕。城市以外的人不会阅读的超本地化新闻媒体充斥着犯罪报道和人们发出的各种警告。甚至几十年前，当地新闻也会兴奋地报道犯罪，因为它吸引观众。

几乎每个大城市的 Reddit 子版块都需要有某种限制犯罪帖子的规则，因为人们喜欢发布发生的每一起犯罪行为，并且最终会接管该子版块。然后创建“替代”子版块并继续循环。

吓唬人总是有利可图的，社交媒体只是降低了门槛。不再只是老年人了。

--

我差点就掉进了这些陷阱之一。一个人在死气沉沉的旧金山购物中心里走来走去，谈论着它是如何被摧毁的，旧金山正在崩溃。事实上我最近去过那里，是的，那里很空。 YouTube 算法抓住了这一点，开始向我展示更多视频。

除了其中一个视频是在我旁边的街道上。这段视频只是一个人绕着两个空荡荡的街区走来走去，但一遍又一遍地循环播放了 20 多分钟。所以这显然是无稽之谈。

我又查了一下他的频道。它有付费订阅和 PayPal 链接。他的每个视频的观看次数至少为 5 万次，有些甚至达到 80 万次。他每个月做两个。那是很轻松的收入。

This isn't limited to far-away places. There are plenty of influencers and social media shills that specifically parrot how bad cities are - to people who live in those cities. Hyper-local news outlets that no one outside of the city would read are filled with crime reports and people giving all kinds of warnings. Even decades ago, local news would excitedly report on crime because it draws viewers.

Almost every single large city subreddit needs to have some kinds of rules restricting crime posts, because people love to post every single act of crime that happens and it eventually takes over the sub. Then "alternative" subreddits are created and the cycle continues.

Scaring people has always been profitable, social media just lowers the bar. It's not just old people anymore.

--

I almost fell into one of these traps. It was a guy walking around the dead San Francisco mall and talking about how it was DEVASTATED and SF was COLLAPSING. I had actually been there recently and yes, it was pretty empty. The Youtube algorithm seized on this and started showing me more videos.

Except one of the videos was on a street next to me. The video was just the one guy walking around two blocks that were empty, but looped over and over for 20+ minutes. So it was clearly nonsense.

I checked his channel again. It has paid subscriptions and a PayPal link. He gets minimum 50K views on every single video, with some hitting 800K. He makes two a month. That's an easy income.

pojzon 2026/04/07 05:00
作为东欧公民，我认为绝对没有理由像“以往”那样访问西欧。

每次我因商务或家庭旅行而访问时，我们要么被抢劫，要么被纠缠，要么被咒骂。

也许参观小村庄更好，但大城市就像文明人的“禁区”。

你可能不同意，但我的这个观点是根据自己的经验得出的，没有什么可以改变它。

As a Eastern Europe citizen, I see absolutely no reason to visit western europe like “ever”.

Every time I did visit due to business or family trip, we were either mugged or pestered or cursed at.

Maybe visiting small villages is better but main cities are like “no-go” zones for civilized ppl.

You may not agree but I built this opinion on own experience and nothing will change it.

mhh__ 2026/04/07 04:17
反过来，我厌倦了短视的傻瓜利用外国的愚蠢来掩盖不可否认的事实，即伦敦存在轻微犯罪和反社会行为的巨大问题。玛莎百货的老板最近就此事写了一封信

https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/apr/03/marks-spenc...

> 我不断听说犯罪率正在下降，尤其是在伦敦——我们没有人相信这一点，也很少有从事零售业的人会看到这一点。事实上，我们在商业街和商店中看到的情况完全相反，我们的同事每天都在工作场所遭受虐待和暴力。

> 它变得更加厚颜无耻、更有组织性、更具攻击性。

> 去年，英国各地发生了约 550 万起商店行窃事件，其中不包括大量未报告的事件。每天，超过 1,600 名零售工人面临暴力或虐待。这并不是孤立的。这是系统性的，而且正在变得更糟，而不是更好。

In turn I'm sick of myopic fools using foreign stupidity to cover up the undeniable truth that there is a huge problem with petty crime and anti-social behaviour in london e.g. the boss of M&S wrote a letter on this just recently

https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/apr/03/marks-spenc...

> I keep hearing crime is falling, especially in London - something none of us believe and very few people working in retail would see. In fact, we see the absolute opposite in our High Streets and in our stores, where our colleagues are on the receiving end of abuse and violence in their workplace every day.

> It is becoming more brazen, more organised and more aggressive.

> Across the UK, there were around 5.5 million incidents of shoplifting last year, and that excludes the vast number that go unreported. Every day, more than 1,600 retail workers face violence or abuse. This is not isolated. It is systemic and it is getting worse, not better.
